mission.title: istio: Multi-Cluster/Multi-Network - Cannot use a hostname-based gateway for east-west traffic
mission.description: Multi-Cluster/Multi-Network - Cannot use a hostname-based gateway for east-west traffic. Requested by 20+ users.

steps.title: Check current istio deployment
steps.description:
Verify your istio version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
helm list -n istio 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working istio installation.

steps.title: Review istio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant istio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get configmap -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=istio
```
**Bug description**

Following the guide [Install Multi-Primary on different networks](https://istio.io/latest/docs/setup/install/multicluster/multi-primary_multi-network), everything seems to install as expected without errors and is running in the

steps.title: Apply the fix for Multi-Cluster/Multi-Network - Cannot use a hostname-based…
steps.description:
Hi  

This looks as though your clusters are not properly configured for endpoint discovery. This requires both eastwest gateway and as well the secrets to be properly configured.

To verify secrets, check if you have the kubeconfigs located in the `istio-system` namespace for both clusters. And
```yaml
# From Cluster 1 [where helloworld v1 is deployed]
$ while true; do kubectl exec --context="${CTX_CLUSTER1}" -n sample -c sleep     "$(kubectl get pod --context="${CTX_CLUSTER1}" -n sample -l \
    app=sleep -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')"     -- curl -s helloworld.sample:5000/hello; done
Hello version: v1, instance: helloworld-v1-578dd69f69-r9lkz
Hello version: v1, instance: helloworld-v1-578dd69f69-r9lkz
Hello version: v1, instance: helloworld-v1-578dd69f69-r9lkz
Hello version: v1, instance: helloworld-v1-578dd69f69-r9lkz
Hello version: v1, instance: helloworld-v1-578dd69f69-r9lkz

```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get events -n istio --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Multi-Cluster/Multi-Network - Cannot use a hostname-based…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hi  

This looks as though your clusters are not properly configured for endpoint discovery. This requires both eastwest gateway and as well the secrets to be properly configured.

To verify secrets, check if you have the kubeconfigs located in the `istio-system` namespace for both clusters.
codeSnippets:
# From Cluster 1 [where helloworld v1 is deployed]
$ while true; do kubectl exec --context="${CTX_CLUSTER1}" -n sample -c sleep     "$(kubectl get pod --context="${CTX_CLUSTER1}" -n sample -l \
    app=sleep -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')"     -- curl -s helloworld.sample:5000/hello; done
Hello version: v1, instance: helloworld-v1-578dd69f69-r9lkz

...

$ while true; do kubectl exec --context="${CTX_CLUSTER2}" -n sample -c sleep     "$(kubectl get pod --context="${CTX_CLUSTER2}" -n sample -l \

# Cluster 1
$ istioctl -n sample --context=${CTX_CLUSTER1} proxy-config endpoint "$(kubectl get pod --context="${CTX_CLUSTER1}" -n sample -l app=sleep -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')" | grep helloworld

# Cluster 2
$ istioctl -n sample --context=${CTX_CLUSTER2} proxy-config endpoint "$(kubectl get pod --context="${CTX_CLUSTER2}" -n sample -l app=sleep -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')" | grep helloworld
10.100.2.188:5000                HEALTHY     OK                outbound|5000||helloworld.sample.svc.cluster.local

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with istio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
